topic: iran us tensions escalate with drone strike and retaliation fears
headline_marked: [[22 Countries Warn Iran]] to stop attacks 'on our soil' amid escalating tensions
headline: 22 Countries Warn Iran to stop attacks 'on our soil' amid escalating tensions
summary: A joint statement from the US and European nations condemns Iran's use of criminal gangs for plots abroad and demands an immediate halt to lethal actions against dissidents and communities.
details: Twenty-two countries, including the United States and several European nations, have issued a stern warning to Iran, demanding an immediate cessation of attacks targeting individuals on their soil. The joint statement condemned Iran's security services for their "deplorable" use of international and local criminal gangs for plots in Europe, North America, and Australia.

The countries asserted that Iran's actions, which include attempts to kill, kidnap, harass, or intimidate people, undermine national sovereignty and international norms. Specific accusations were leveled against Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' intelligence services and its Quds Force for alleged "lethal plotting and malign actions" against Iranian dissidents, journalists, and Jewish and Israeli communities.

The statement emphasized a united front in protecting their nations and people against these threats, declaring, "The Islamic Republic of Iran must halt these actions now." This warning comes amid a backdrop of escalating tensions and exchanges of attacks between Iran and the US.

Iran has also been accused of orchestrating a campaign of attacks across Europe targeting Jewish communities, Iranian journalists, and US journalists. These attacks were claimed by a group linked to Iran, Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamiya (HAYI), which means "The Islamic Movement of the People of the Right Hand."

HAYI has reportedly claimed responsibility for incidents such as the stabbing of two Jewish men and arson attacks on synagogues and community sites in north London. Australia previously expelled Iran's ambassador and suspended embassy operations after accusing Tehran of directing antisemitic attacks, including an arson attack on a synagogue in Melbourne and the torching of a kosher cafe in Sydney.

In response to these alleged actions, Australia designated the Revolutionary Guards as a state sponsor of terrorism last November, labeling the incidents as "unprecedented and dangerous acts of aggression orchestrated by a foreign nation on Australian soil." Iran's foreign ministry dismissed Australia's decision as an "insulting and unjustified act."
caption:
Twenty-two countries, including the US and European nations, have jointly warned Iran to stop attacking people "on our soil."

Twenty-two countries, including the United States and several European nations, have issued a stern warning to Iran, demanding an immediate cessation of attacks targeting individuals on their soil. The joint statement condemned Iran's security services for their "deplorable" use of international and local criminal gangs for plots in Europe, North America, and Australia.

The countries asserted that Iran's actions, which include attempts to kill, kidnap, harass, or intimidate people, undermine national sovereignty and international norms. Specific accusations were leveled against Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' intelligence services and its Quds Force for alleged "lethal plotting and malign actions" against Iranian dissidents, journalists, and Jewish and Israeli communities.

The statement emphasized a united front in protecting their nations and people against these threats, declaring, "The Islamic Republic of Iran must halt these actions now." This warning comes amid a backdrop of escalating tensions and exchanges of attacks between Iran and the US.

Iran has also been accused of orchestrating a campaign of attacks across Europe targeting Jewish communities, Iranian journalists, and US journalists. These attacks were claimed by a group linked to Iran, Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamiya (HAYI), which means "The Islamic Movement of the People of the Right Hand."

HAYI has reportedly claimed responsibility for incidents such as the stabbing of two Jewish men and arson attacks on synagogues and community sites in north London. Australia previously expelled Iran's ambassador and suspended embassy operations after accusing Tehran of directing antisemitic attacks, including an arson attack on a synagogue in Melbourne and the torching of a kosher cafe in Sydney.

Source: Jamuna TV, The Daily Star, Somoy News

topic: bangladesh air pollution worsens Dhaka ranks second globally
headline_marked: Dhaka ranks second globally for air pollution, despite recent rain
headline: Dhaka ranks second globally for air pollution, despite recent rain
summary: Despite recent rainfall which usually improves air quality, Dhaka has been ranked the second most polluted city globally. The air quality index (AQI) in the capital reached 167 on Thursday morning, categorised as 'unhealthy'.
details: Dhaka ranked second among 122 cities worldwide for air pollution on Thursday morning, with an Air Quality Index (AQI) of 167, classified as 'unhealthy'. This level of pollution can cause respiratory illnesses.

The ranking comes as a surprise, as the city experienced significant rainfall on Wednesday, with 31 millimeters recorded in two hours. Typically, rain improves air quality, and June is usually a period of lower pollution levels in Dhaka.

Kampala, Uganda, topped the list with an AQI of 173. Globally, air pollution levels are generally lower than usual for this time of year. Air quality tends to improve after April, especially with rainfall.

Eight specific areas in Dhaka recorded particularly poor air quality. Agargaon was the most polluted, with an AQI of 191. Other highly polluted areas included Abdullahbag in North Badda (174), Grace International School in Gulshan (173), and various locations in Baridhara and Gulshan.
An AQI between 150 and 199 is considered 'unhealthy', while an AQI of 200 or above is deemed 'very unhealthy'.

IQAir, a Swiss organization that monitors air quality, provided the data and offered recommendations for residents. They advised wearing masks outdoors, especially for sensitive groups, and avoiding outdoor exercise.
Residents were also advised to keep their windows closed as much as possible to minimize indoor exposure to the polluted air.
caption:
Dhaka ranked second most polluted city globally today, with an AQI of 167, despite recent rainfall. Eight areas in the capital show particularly poor air quality.

Dhaka ranked second among 122 cities worldwide for air pollution on Thursday morning, with an Air Quality Index (AQI) of 167, classified as 'unhealthy'. This level of pollution can cause respiratory illnesses.

The ranking comes as a surprise, as the city experienced significant rainfall on Wednesday, with 31 millimeters recorded in two hours. Typically, rain improves air quality, and June is usually a period of lower pollution levels in Dhaka.

Kampala, Uganda, topped the list with an AQI of 173. Globally, air pollution levels are generally lower than usual for this time of year. Air quality tends to improve after April, especially with rainfall.

Eight specific areas in Dhaka recorded particularly poor air quality. Agargaon was the most polluted, with an AQI of 191. Other highly polluted areas included Abdullahbag in North Badda (174), Grace International School in Gulshan (173), and various locations in Baridhara and Gulshan.

An AQI between 150 and 199 is considered 'unhealthy', while an AQI of 200 or above is deemed 'very unhealthy'.

IQAir, a Swiss organization that monitors air quality, provided the data and offered recommendations for residents. They advised wearing masks outdoors, especially for sensitive groups, and avoiding outdoor exercise.

Residents were also advised to keep their windows closed as much as possible to minimize indoor exposure to the polluted air.

#Dhaka #AirPollution #Bangladesh #Pollution #Environment

Source: Prothom Alo, Somoy News, Dhaka Tribune
tweet: Dhaka ranks second globally for air pollution with an AQI of 167, classified as 'unhealthy'. Despite recent rain, pollution levels remain high in several areas. Residents advised to wear masks outdoors. #Dhaka #AirPollution
source: Prothom Alo, Somoy News, Dhaka Tribune
